issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer's 7 inner result classes form the data contract consumed by the api layer, coupling api to query internals
evidence: WordSketchApiServer imports pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions (lines 16-21).

SemanticFieldExplorer Javadoc: 'This class also hosts the result DTOs... These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585).' — the debt is acknowledged but unresolved.
WordSketchApiServer holds 'private final SemanticFieldExplorer semanticFieldExplorer' — direct coupling to concrete query class from api layer.
issues.suggestion: Extract ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge, ExploreOptions into a dedicated pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package. Update imports in both SemanticFieldExplorer and WordSketchApiServer. This severs the api→query inner class dependency and makes the data contract independent of the class that computes it.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer is a 988-line god class mixing route registration, request parsing, business logic, and response building
evidence: 988 lines with 10 private handler methods: handleSketchRequest, handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery, handleRelationQueryInternal, handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticField, handleSemanticFieldExamples, handleConcordanceExamples, handleVisualRadial, handleBcqlQueryPost.
Each handler method performs parameter extraction, validation, business-logic invocation, and response-map assembly. None of these concerns is separately testable without starting an HTTP server.
handleSemanticFieldExplore alone is ~120 lines (lines 437-557) building a response map field by field.
issues.suggestion: Extract request parsing and response building into dedicated handler classes or at minimum extract a response builder per logical feature area (SketchResponseBuilder, SemanticFieldResponseBuilder). Route registration belongs in a separate configuration method or a dedicated Router class. Each handler should receive already-parsed parameters and return a domain result, not a raw Map.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer owns 7 public inner result classes that serve as the api/query data contract, blurring class ownership
evidence: 7 public static inner classes defined inside SemanticFieldExplorer: ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge, ExploreOptions.
The class Javadoc explicitly acknowledges this: 'These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585).'
WordSketchApiServer imports all 6 of these types to build API responses — the api layer's data shapes are governed by a class in the query package.
issues.suggestion: Create a pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package and move all 7 inner classes there as top-level types. SemanticFieldExplorer returns model types; WordSketchApiServer imports from model, not query.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor exposes findDependencyCollocations and executeDependencyPattern that are not on QueryExecutor, leaking concrete-type dependency to callers
evidence: findDependencyCollocations (line 139 of BlackLabQueryExecutor) is public but not declared on QueryExecutor.
executeDependencyPattern (line 364 of BlackLabQueryExecutor) is public but not on QueryExecutor.
Main.java calls executor.findDependencyCollocations(...) after casting the QueryExecutor reference to BlackLabQueryExecutor — the interface does not express the full contract.
issues.suggestion: Add findDependencyCollocations and executeDependencyPattern to the QueryExecutor interface (with proper Javadoc), or — if dependency queries are truly BlackLab-specific — introduce a sub-interface DependencyQueryExecutor that BlackLabQueryExecutor implements, so the type hierarchy reflects the actual capability boundary.

issues.summary: ConcordanceResult is a wide context bag with 7 nullable fields requiring callers to understand internal production paths
evidence: ConcordanceResult has 11 fields: sentence, rawXml, lemma, tag, word, startOffset, endOffset, docId, collocateLemma, frequency, logDice.

Javadoc documents 5 complex nullability conditions: rawXml present only for BCQL executor; lemma/tag/word null for grouped collocate results; docId may be null; collocateLemma present only for grouped results; frequency/logDice meaningful only when collocateLemma is non-null.
The forSnippet factory method sets 5 fields to null and 2 to 0, confirming the bag pattern — multiple distinct result shapes are collapsed into one class.
issues.suggestion: Split ConcordanceResult into two or three dedicated types: SnippetResult (sentence, startOffset, endOffset, docId), TokenResult (extends SnippetResult adding lemma, tag, word, rawXml), and CollocateResult (lemma, frequency, logDice, examples). Use sealed classes or a common interface. Each type would have no nullable fields and a clear use case.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser has 4 deprecated wrapper methods that are still called by production code, creating a live double-indirection layer
evidence: extractCollocateFromSnippet is @Deprecated but called from BlackLabQueryExecutor.extractDependentLemmaFromGroup (line 432) and extractCollocateLemma (the new clean-API wrapper) delegates to it.
extractCollocateFromMatch and extractCollocateFromPlainText are @Deprecated but still called from executeSurfacePattern (lines 480-483).

The 'Consolidated clean API' section (extractLemmaAt, extractHeadLemma, extractCollocateLemma) at the bottom of the file wraps the deprecated methods without replacing them — two parallel APIs exist simultaneously.
issues.suggestion: Complete the migration: replace all callers of the 4 deprecated methods with the clean-API equivalents (extractLemmaAt, extractHeadLemma, extractCollocateLemma), then remove the deprecated methods. If the clean API doesn't cover all cases, extend it rather than calling deprecated methods from the new layer.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) constructor creates a BlackLabQueryExecutor internally, documented as creating double-open risk
evidence: Constructor: 'public SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) throws IOException { ... this.executor = new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath); this.ownsExecutor = true; }'
Javadoc warns: 'Prefer SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) (dependency injection) for testability and to avoid double-open of the same index (see issue 297b2b52).'
The ownsExecutor boolean flag (and the associated close logic) adds complexity that exists only because of this constructor.
issues.suggestion: Remove the String-path constructor. All callers that need a SemanticFieldExplorer should obtain a QueryExecutor externally and inject it. The ownsExecutor field and its conditional close() logic can then be removed.

issues.summary: com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer is a JDK internal API used as the production HTTP server, risking portability and longevity
evidence: WordSketchApiServer declares 'private com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer server;' (line 56) and creates it via 'com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer.create(new InetSocketAddress(port), 0)' (line 74).
Handler registration uses 'com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer httpServer' as parameter type (line 185) and 'com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpHandler' as the handler type (line 186).

com.sun.* packages are internal JDK packages, not part of the public Java SE API. While widely available in practice, they are not guaranteed across all JDK vendors (e.g., GraalVM native image, IBM J9) and have been subject to removal or encapsulation in past JDK releases.
issues.suggestion: Replace com.sun.net.httpserver with a proper lightweight HTTP server dependency such as Javalin (which embeds Jetty), Eclipse Jetty embedded, or — if zero added dependencies is preferred — use the jdk.httpserver module explicitly declared in module-info.java (Java 9+ makes the server available via jdk.httpserver module, which is at least explicitly declared rather than a com.sun internal). Add the chosen server as a dependency in pom.xml so the dependency is explicit and version-pinned.

issues.summary: handleSurfaceRelations and handleDepRelations are near-identical (~40 lines each), differing only in RelationType filter and one extra field
evidence: handleSurfaceRelations (lines 236-255): iterates relations, filters by RelationType.SURFACE, builds Map with id/name/description/relation_type/pattern keys.
handleDepRelations (lines 257-277): identical structure, filters by RelationType.DEP, adds one extra 'deprel' key.
Both methods have the same null-check on grammarConfig and the same sendJsonResponse call.
issues.suggestion: Extract a private buildRelationsList(RelationType type, boolean includeDeprel) method and call it from both handlers, or unify into a single handleRelations(RelationType type) method.

issues.summary: handleFullSketch and handleFullDependencySketch are near-duplicates (~50 lines each) sharing the same relation-iteration and collocation-assembly structure
evidence: handleFullSketch (lines 279-322) and handleFullDependencySketch (lines 333-384) both: iterate grammarConfig.getRelations(), filter by a RelationType, call executeSurfacePattern with the same parameters, build a collocations list with the same Map structure (lemma/frequency/logDice/pos keys).
The inner collocations-assembly loop (lines 299-308 vs 361-368) is character-for-character identical.
The only structural difference: handleFullDependencySketch adds id/name/description/deprel/total_matches to the relation-level map.
issues.suggestion: Extract the shared collocation-assembly loop into a private List<Map<String,Object>> buildCollocations(List<WordSketchResult>) method. Unify the two handlers into a single handleFullSketch(exchange, lemma, RelationType) method with conditional extras for DEP.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExplore assembles a 120-line response by repeating the same Map-put-loop pattern four times with no extraction
evidence: Lines 501-554: four consecutive loop blocks each creating a list of Maps, each following the same structure: 'List<Map<...>> x = new ArrayList<>(); for (Y y : result.getYs()) { Map<...> m = new HashMap<>(); m.put(...); ... x.add(m); } response.put(..., x);'
The same pattern (collocMap.put, nounMap.put, collocMap.put, edgeMap.put) is repeated without any helper or DTO serializer.
The equivalent response-building in handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti (lines 658-688) duplicates two of the four loops.
issues.suggestion: Introduce a private static Map<String,Object> toCollocMap(WordSketchResult), toNounMap(DiscoveredNoun), toCoreCollocMap(CoreCollocate), toEdgeMap(Edge) method per result type. Each handler calls these helpers instead of inlining the field assignments.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations silently discards the provided cqlPattern in the else-fallback branch
evidence: Lines 78-89: if (cqlPattern.contains('%s')) → format; else if (cqlPattern.startsWith('[')) → prepend headword; else → 'bcql = String.format("\"%s\" []", headword.toLowerCase())' which ignores cqlPattern entirely.
A caller passing a non-template, non-bracket-starting pattern (e.g., a quoted string or a multi-token pattern) would silently receive results for 'headword []' instead of an error.
The comment 'Fallback: just search for headword' does not signal to callers that their input was dropped.
issues.suggestion: Remove the silent fallback. Either throw an IllegalArgumentException for unrecognized pattern forms, or document the three expected forms with @throws in the Javadoc and validate at entry. If '[]' is a valid intent, make it the default value at the call site, not a hidden fallback inside the method.

issues.summary: CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens uses a naive indexOf(']') that fails for ']' inside quoted strings within brackets
evidence: Line 44: 'int end = pattern.indexOf(']', i);' — this finds the first ']' after '[', even if that ']' is inside a quoted attribute value like [lemma="a]b"].
The comment 'Skip quoted string to avoid mistaking embedded content for tokens' only handles standalone quoted strings at the outer level, not quotes nested inside brackets.
A pattern like 1:[xpos="[NN.*]"] would be split incorrectly: the method would return [xpos="[NN.*] as one token, then treat ]"] as leftover text.
issues.suggestion: Rewrite splitCqlTokens to track both bracket nesting depth and whether the scanner is currently inside a double-quoted string (toggling on unescaped '"' characters). The '[' only opens a token when depth == 0, and ']' only closes the current token when depth == 1 and not inside a quote.

issues.summary: API layer imports 6 inner result classes from SemanticFieldExplorer, coupling api to query internals
evidence: WordSketchApiServer lines 13-21 import SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions as named inner classes
SemanticFieldExplorer javadoc (line 55) acknowledges these should move to a dedicated model/ package
issues.suggestion: Move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExploreOptions, and Edge into a new pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package. Update imports in WordSketchApiServer and SemanticFieldExplorer. This severs the api→query inner-class dependency.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti reimplements collocate intersection inline, bypassing SemanticFieldExplorer
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti iterates seeds manually, calls executor.executeSurfacePattern per seed, and computes intersection via commonCollocates.retainAll — all inline (~lines 620-650)
SemanticFieldExplorer.compare() (line 293 of SemanticFieldExplorer) already provides multi-noun comparison with richer output; the multi-seed handler ignores it
semanticFieldExplorer field is available in the handler's class but is not called in handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
issues.suggestion: Add a SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMulti(Set<String> seeds, String relationName, ...) method that encapsulates seed-loop and intersection logic. Have handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti delegate to it, mirroring how handleSemanticFieldExplore delegates to exploreByPattern.

issues.summary: ExploreOptions.executor field is always passed null by all call sites, creating a misleading boundary contract
evidence: Both ExploreOptions construction sites in WordSketchApiServer pass null for the executor parameter (lines ~503 and ~638)
ExploreOptions is a record with an 'executor' field; a caller reading the signature cannot know it is always null in production use
issues.suggestion: Remove the executor field from ExploreOptions if it is always null in practice. If it was intended for override injection, document the expected non-null contract and add a null-guard or assertion in exploreByPattern.

issues.summary: extractLemmaAt/extractHeadLemma/extractCollocateLemma are pass-through aliases with no added logic over the deprecated originals
evidence: extractLemmaAt(xml, pos) is a one-line body: return extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition(xml, position) — same logic, different name
extractHeadLemma(matchXml) calls extractLemmaAt(matchXml, 1) with no guard, transformation, or domain logic
extractCollocateLemma(matchXml) calls the @Deprecated extractCollocateFromSnippet — the 'clean' API delegates to the deprecated one, so callers of the new API still trigger deprecation internally
issues.suggestion: Inline the logic from extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition directly into extractLemmaAt (making extractLemmaAt the canonical implementation), then update extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition to delegate to it instead. Remove extractHeadLemma and extractCollocateLemma if callers can use extractLemmaAt(xml,1) and extractLemmaAt(xml, n) directly, or give them non-trivial behavior (e.g., extractCollocateLemma should determine the collocate position from context, not just forward to a deprecated method).

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern emits 7+ INFO-level trace messages that belong at DEBUG
evidence: Lines ~120-135 log a banner separator, seed, relation, pattern, head/collocate positions, and parameter block all at INFO on every invocation
Further INFO logs at each step ('Step 1: Finding collocates...', '  Found N collocates:', per-collocate lines) create a dense trace stream in production
issues.suggestion: Change the banner and per-step trace messages from logger.info to logger.debug. Keep a single logger.info at entry summarising the operation (e.g., 'Exploring semantic field for seed={} relation={}') and one at exit ('Exploration complete: {} nouns discovered').

issues.summary: handleVisualRadial javadoc repeats 'Render radial plot.' as its first two lines
evidence: The method's opening javadoc block begins with two consecutive 'Render radial plot.' lines before the actual description
issues.suggestion: Remove the duplicate first line from the javadoc.

issues.summary: 3 @Deprecated BlackLabSnippetParser methods remain the active call path in BlackLabQueryExecutor
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor line 325: extractCollocateFromSnippet(matchXml) — the @Deprecated method is called inside extractCollocateLemma helper
BlackLabQueryExecutor line 431: extractCollocateFromSnippet(identity) in extractDependentLemmaFromGroup
BlackLabQueryExecutor line 480: extractCollocateFromMatch(identity, collocateLabelPos) in executeSurfacePattern
BlackLabQueryExecutor line 483: extractCollocateFromPlainText(identity, collocateLabelPos) in executeSurfacePattern
issues.suggestion: Replace each call site with the declared replacement: extractCollocateFromSnippet → extractCollocateLemma(xml) or extractLemmaAt(xml, n); extractCollocateFromMatch → extractLemmaAt(matchOnly, labelPos); extractCollocateFromPlainText → extractCollocateFromMatchText(text, position). Once no callers remain, delete the deprecated methods.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateLemma (the 'clean' replacement) calls the @Deprecated extractCollocateFromSnippet
evidence: extractCollocateLemma(matchXml) body: return extractCollocateFromSnippet(matchXml) — the replacement wraps the deprecated method without migrating the implementation
Callers of the 'new' API trigger the deprecated implementation on every call
issues.suggestion: Move the LEMMA_ATTR regex loop from extractCollocateFromSnippet directly into extractCollocateLemma, then update extractCollocateFromSnippet to delegate to extractCollocateLemma (or delete it). This inverts the delegation direction so the new name owns the implementation.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer inner DTOs are documented as needing a model/ package move but have no completion criteria
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer javadoc lines 54-56: 'These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585)'
WordSketchApiServer TODO(42e2a9a1) depends on that migration completing before its own imports can be cleaned up — two files in a holding pattern
issues.suggestion: Create pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package and move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge, ExploreOptions into it. Update imports in both SemanticFieldExplorer and WordSketchApiServer. Remove both TODO comments once done.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer hosts 7 result DTO inner classes that inflate query/ and force api/ to import query internals
evidence: WordSketchApiServer imports 6 inner classes from SemanticFieldExplorer: ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions
SemanticFieldExplorer.java is 650+ LOC; a significant portion is DTO record/class definitions rather than query logic
issues.suggestion: Stage 1: Create src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/model/ package. Stage 2: Move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge, ExploreOptions into separate files in model/. Stage 3: Update imports in SemanticFieldExplorer and WordSketchApiServer (run `mvn compile` to validate no broken references). Stage 4: Remove the @see note in SemanticFieldExplorer javadoc once done.

issues.summary: utils/ packages four unrelated concerns (CQL parsing, statistics, data structures, domain enum) with no organizing theme
evidence: CqlUtils parses CQL token syntax; LogDiceCalculator computes association scores; LongIntHashMap is a generic off-heap map; PosGroup is a domain enum for POS grouping — no shared concern across these four files
PatternSubstitution in api/ depends primarily on CqlUtils, suggesting CqlUtils has stronger affinity with pattern-processing utilities than with the api/ layer where PatternSubstitution lives
issues.suggestion: Consider splitting utils/ into: scoring/ (LogDiceCalculator), collections/ or keeping LongIntHashMap in utils/ as a genuine general utility, and moving PosGroup into config/ (it is a domain concept used by GrammarConfigLoader). CqlUtils could move to a grammar/ or parsing/ sub-package alongside PatternSubstitution. Run `mvn compile` after each move to catch import breaks.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer constructs SemanticFieldExplorer directly in its constructor, blocking injection and contradicting SEF's own DI recommendation
evidence: WordSketchApiServer constructor line 67: 'this.semanticFieldExplorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor)' — SemanticFieldExplorer is created unconditionally from the injected executor

SemanticFieldExplorer.java lines 93-98 Javadoc: 'Prefer SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) (dependency injection) for testability and to avoid double-open of the same index' — the recommended approach is injection, but WordSketchApiServer bypasses this by constructing directly
Tests that want to inject a mock SemanticFieldExplorer cannot do so without modifying WordSketchApiServer or subclassing it
issues.suggestion: Add a second WordSketchApiServer constructor that accepts SemanticFieldExplorer as a parameter: 'public WordSketchApiServer(QueryExecutor executor, String indexPath, int port, GrammarConfigLoader grammarConfig, SemanticFieldExplorer sef)'. Have the existing 4-arg constructor delegate to it by calling 'new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor)' as the default. This preserves backward compatibility while enabling injection in tests.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer mixes server lifecycle, routing, request parsing, business dispatch, and JSON serialization in one 988-line class
evidence: 10+ private handler methods each contain their own parameter extraction, validation, executor call, and Map<String,Object> serialization — no separation of concerns between these layers.
start() both registers routes (side effect) and starts the server — acknowledged in TODO comment 595bb84c.
issues.suggestion: Extract a RequestContext/ResponseSerializer layer (or typed DTO classes) for JSON building. Move route registration to a separate registerRoutes() method. Consider splitting into RequestDispatcher + individual RequestHandler classes, one per endpoint group.

issues.summary: Parameter parsing and numeric validation block is duplicated across handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
evidence: Both handlers contain: resolveRelationAlias, grammarConfig null check, getRelation lookup, and try-catch block parsing top/min_shared/min_logdice with NumberFormatException — roughly 20 lines duplicated verbatim.
The only difference is that explore-multi also parses seeds while explore parses seed + nouns_per.
issues.suggestion: Extract a SemanticFieldParams record and a parseSemanticFieldParams(Map<String,String> params, boolean multiSeed) helper that returns the parsed record or sends a 400 and returns null.

issues.summary: Six result DTOs are inner classes of SemanticFieldExplorer, forcing the API layer to import implementation internals
evidence: WordSketchApiServer imports: SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions — 6 inner-class imports from an implementation class.
SemanticFieldExplorer Javadoc acknowledges: 'These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585)' — the known issue has not been addressed.
issues.suggestion: Move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExploreOptions, and Edge to a dedicated pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package. Update all import sites.

issues.summary: POST handlers (handleVisualRadial, handleBcqlQueryPost) re-implement OPTIONS/method-check logic already encapsulated in registerGetHandler
evidence: handleVisualRadial: 'if (OPTIONS.equalsIgnoreCase(method)) { sendOptionsResponse(...); return; } if (!requireMethod(exchange, POST)) { return; }' — 4 lines that mirror registerGetHandler's pattern.
handleBcqlQueryPost duplicates the same 4 lines.
registerGetHandler exists precisely to centralize this cross-cutting concern, but POST endpoints bypass it because it hardcodes 'GET'.
issues.suggestion: Add a registerHandler(server, path, method, handler) overload to registerGetHandler (renaming it) that takes the HTTP method as a parameter; use it for both GET and POST registrations.

issues.summary: getTotalFrequency declares throws IOException but silently returns 0L on all failures, corrupting logDice scores
evidence: Method signature: 'public long getTotalFrequency(String lemma) throws IOException'.
Implementation: catch (Exception e) { logger.warn(...); return 0L; } — all exceptions are swallowed.
Javadoc explicitly states: 'Note: a 0 return from a failed lookup will silently corrupt logDice scores for the calling context.' — the violation is known and documented but not fixed.
issues.suggestion: Either propagate the exception (remove the catch, let IOException bubble) so callers can handle lookup failures, or change the declared return type contract to Optional<Long> / return -1 as a sentinel that callers explicitly handle. If silent-0 is intentional for resilience, remove 'throws IOException' from the signature to match the true contract.

issues.summary: ExploreOptions.executor is typed as QueryExecutor but is always null and never read by exploreByPattern
evidence: ExploreOptions record declaration: 'public record ExploreOptions(int topCollocates, int nounsPerCollocate, double minLogDice, int minShared, boolean includeExamples, int headPos, int collocatePos, QueryExecutor executor)'.
All call sites pass null as the executor: 'new ExploreOptions(topCollocates, nounsPerCollocate, minLogDice, minShared, false, headPos, collocatePos, null)'.
exploreByPattern never references opts.executor() — it uses this.executor throughout.
issues.suggestion: Remove the executor field from ExploreOptions. If per-call executor injection is ever needed, add it as a method parameter to exploreByPattern directly rather than as a record field.

issues.summary: handleSemanticField parses numeric query params without NumberFormatException handling, unlike sibling handlers
evidence: 'double minLogDice = Double.parseDouble(params.getOrDefault("min_logdice", "3.0"));' and 'int maxPerNoun = Integer.parseInt(params.getOrDefault("max_per_noun", "50"));' — no try/catch.

handleSemanticFieldExplore wraps equivalent parsing in: 'try { topCollocates = Integer.parseInt(...); ... } catch (NumberFormatException e) { HttpApiUtils.sendError(exchange, 400, ...); return; }' — the contract for callers differs between identical operations on sibling endpoints.
issues.suggestion: Wrap the Double.parseDouble / Integer.parseInt calls in handleSemanticField in the same try/catch pattern used by handleSemanticFieldExplore, returning 400 on parse failure.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateLemma delegates to deprecated extractCollocateFromSnippet, blurring the deprecation boundary
evidence: 'static String extractCollocateLemma(String matchXml) { return extractCollocateFromSnippet(matchXml); }' — the 'clean API' method is a thin wrapper that forwards to the method it is meant to replace.

extractCollocateFromSnippet is marked @Deprecated with 'Use extractHeadLemma or extractCollocateLemma for clarity' — but extractCollocateLemma itself calls the deprecated method, making both names aliases for the same behavior rather than a meaningful replacement.
issues.suggestion: Inline the implementation of extractCollocateFromSnippet into extractCollocateLemma (iterate LEMMA_ATTR, return last group). Then the deprecated method can delegate to the new one (reversing the delegation direction) or be deleted if no external callers remain.

issues.summary: findCollocations silently discards the provided cqlPattern when it neither contains '%s' nor starts with '['
evidence: The three-branch condition: if (cqlPattern.contains('%s')) { ... } else if (cqlPattern.startsWith('[')) { ... } else { bcql = String.format('"headword" []', ...); } — the else branch ignores cqlPattern entirely.

Any caller passing a pattern like '[lemma="theory"] [xpos="JJ.*"]' (starts with '[', contains lemma constraint) would have their pattern used; but '(lemma=theory) [xpos="JJ.*"]' or a named-pattern form would silently fall to the else branch.
No log message, no exception — callers receive results that look valid but were produced from a different query than requested.
issues.suggestion: Throw IllegalArgumentException (or log WARN and fall back explicitly) when the pattern does not match any expected form. If the 'else' branch is intentional as a default, add a comment explaining the intent and log the substitution at DEBUG level.

issues.summary: scoreHits computes maxResults * 3 without overflow guard, which wraps negative for large maxResults values
evidence: 'int limit = Math.min(records.size(), maxResults * 3);' — maxResults is an int. For maxResults >= 715,828,000, maxResults * 3 overflows to a negative number.
Math.min(records.size(), negative) returns negative, then the loop 'for (int i = 0; i < limit; i++)' never executes, silently returning an empty result list.
issues.suggestion: Use 'int limit = (int) Math.min((long) records.size(), (long) maxResults * 3);' or clamp maxResults before multiplication: 'int safeMax = Math.min(maxResults, Integer.MAX_VALUE / 3); int limit = Math.min(records.size(), safeMax * 3);'

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti assigns the first seed's live HashSet as the intersection base without copying it
evidence: 'if (commonCollocates == null) { commonCollocates = seedCollocates; }' — commonCollocates points to the same Set object as seedCollocates for the first seed.

Subsequent retainAll calls mutate this set in place. The logic works only because seedCollocates is a transient local variable that is not referenced after assignment. A future refactor that captures seedCollocates (e.g., for per-seed reporting) would introduce a subtle bug.
issues.suggestion: Replace with 'commonCollocates = new HashSet<>(seedCollocates);' on the first iteration to make the copy explicit and the logic self-documenting.

issues.summary: exploreByPattern fallback to findCollocations uses the class constant NOUN_PATTERN instead of the caller-supplied simplePattern
evidence: Method receives 'String simplePattern' as parameter — documented as 'Simple pattern for reverse lookup'.
Fallback: 'seedRelations = executor.findCollocations(seed, simplePattern, minLogDice, topPredicates);' — this correctly uses simplePattern.

However, Step 2 inside the method uses the hardcoded class constant: 'List<QueryResults.WordSketchResult> nouns = executor.findCollocations(colloc, NOUN_PATTERN, minLogDice, nounsPerPredicate);' — the caller's simplePattern is ignored for the reverse lookup phase, always using [xpos="NN.*"] regardless of relation type.
issues.suggestion: If the reverse-lookup pattern should always be NOUN_PATTERN regardless of the relation, document this explicitly in the method contract. If it should respect the caller's pattern, use simplePattern in the Step 2 findCollocations call.

issues.summary: All HTTP responses are built as Map<String, Object> with stringly-typed field names; no typed response DTOs exist
evidence: handleFullSketch: 'Map<String, Object> patternData = new HashMap<>(); patternData.put("name", rel.name()); patternData.put("cql", rel.pattern()); ...' — 10+ handler methods each build response maps inline.

Field names like 'seed_collocates_count', 'discovered_nouns_count', 'core_collocates' are plain string literals with no compile-time verification — a typo ('seed_collocate_count' vs 'seed_collocates_count') would silently produce a missing field in the response.
issues.suggestion: Introduce typed response record classes (e.g., SketchResponse, SemanticFieldResponse) serializable with fastjson2 annotations. This makes field names compile-time constants and enables IDE-assisted refactoring.

issues.summary: ConcordanceResult has 7 nullable fields with no @Nullable annotations — null contracts are documentation-only
evidence: Class Javadoc documents nullable fields: rawXml, lemma, tag, word, docId, collocateLemma — but no @Nullable annotation is present on any constructor parameter or getter.
All getters return their fields directly without null-safety indication, meaning callers must read documentation to know which fields are safe to use without null checks.
issues.suggestion: Add @Nullable (from javax.annotation or org.jetbrains.annotations) to the nullable constructor parameters and corresponding getters; add @NonNull to sentence, startOffset, endOffset. This enables IDE nullability analysis and makes contracts machine-verifiable.

issues.summary: scoreHits defaults absent collocate frequency to 1L instead of 0L, silently inflating logDice for unseen collocates
evidence: 'long f_xy = (collocateLemma != null && !collocateLemma.isEmpty()) ? collocateFreqMap.getOrDefault(collocateLemma.toLowerCase(), 1L) : 0L;'

A collocate seen in a hit but absent from the freq map (should not happen in the normal flow) receives f_xy=1 instead of 0, giving it a non-zero logDice score that misrepresents the association strength.
issues.suggestion: Change the default to 0L. If a collocateLemma is truly observed but absent from freqMapOut (a logic inconsistency), log a warning rather than silently treating it as frequency 1.

issues.summary: findDependencyCollocations is a public method absent from QueryExecutor, forcing callers to cast to the concrete type
evidence: Main.java: 'var results = deprel != null ? executor.findDependencyCollocations(lemma, deprel, minLogDice, limit) : executor.findCollocations(...)' — executor is typed as BlackLabQueryExecutor (concrete), not QueryExecutor.
QueryExecutor interface has no findDependencyCollocations method, so the type system cannot enforce this capability on alternative implementations.
Switching to a different QueryExecutor implementation would silently break the CLI command at runtime.
issues.suggestion: Add findDependencyCollocations(String headword, String deprel, double minLogDice, int maxResults) to the QueryExecutor interface, with a default implementation that throws UnsupportedOperationException for backends that do not support dependency queries.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser retains five deprecated methods with overlapping 'extractCollocate*' names alongside new clean-API replacements, creating an unnavigable naming cluster
evidence: Deprecated: extractCollocateFromSnippet, extractCollocateFromMatch, extractCollocateFromPlainText, extractCollocateFromMatchText, extractTokenFromSnippet.
Replacements: extractLemmaAt, extractHeadLemma, extractCollocateLemma — but extractCollocateLemma delegates to the deprecated extractCollocateFromSnippet, so the replacement is a thin alias.
A developer reading the class for the first time sees 10 extraction methods, 5 deprecated, with similar names and no clear grouping.
issues.suggestion: Delete the deprecated methods (they are package-private and only called internally). Inline their implementations into the clean-API methods (extractLemmaAt, extractHeadLemma, extractCollocateLemma). If deletion is not yet possible, add a package-level note indicating the deprecation timeline.

issues.summary: ExploreOptions.executor field name implies an injectable executor but the field is always null and never read
evidence: public record ExploreOptions(..., QueryExecutor executor) — 'executor' is a well-understood term that implies the field is used for query execution.
All call sites: 'new ExploreOptions(..., null)'. exploreByPattern() never calls opts.executor().
A developer reading ExploreOptions would reasonably assume the executor is respected, and might spend time debugging why injecting a mock executor has no effect.
issues.suggestion: Remove the executor field from ExploreOptions entirely (see contract_coherence finding explore_options_dead_executor_param). If the field was intended for future use, add a comment explaining this rather than declaring a typed parameter that is silently ignored.

issues.summary: findDependencyCollocations and executeDependencyPattern have different verbs implying different abstraction levels but both execute BCQL queries returning WordSketchResult
evidence: findDependencyCollocations(headword, deprel, minLogDice, maxResults) — 'find' suggests a high-level search.
executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, headPosConstraint, minLogDice, maxResults) — 'execute' suggests a lower-level execution step.

Both methods internally build a BCQL pattern string and call CorpusQueryLanguageParser.parse() + query.toQuery() + groupWithStoredHits. The 'execute' method adds a headPosConstraint parameter but otherwise uses the same pipeline.
The naming divergence misleads readers into thinking one method is a wrapper around the other, or that they use different execution mechanisms.
issues.suggestion: Unify the naming: either rename findDependencyCollocations to executeDependencyPattern (removing the headPosConstraint overload as a separate method), or rename both to findDependencyCollocations with an optional headPosConstraint parameter. Consistent 'find*Collocations' naming matches the interface vocabulary.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer class Javadoc references 'compareSeeds' but the method is named 'compare'
evidence:
Class-level Javadoc: '

Comparison Mode (multi-seed)

... See {@link #compareSeeds}.'
Actual method signature: 'public ComparisonResult compare(Set<String> seedNouns, double minLogDice, int maxPerNoun)'.
No method named 'compareSeeds' exists in the class — the link would fail javadoc validation.
issues.suggestion: Either rename the method to compareSeeds (aligning code with documentation) or update the Javadoc link to '{@link #compare}'. Renaming is preferable as 'compareSeeds' is more specific than 'compare'.

issues.summary: handleSemanticField, handleSemanticFieldExamples, and handleConcordanceExamples parse numeric URL params without NumberFormatException guard, unlike their sibling handlers
evidence: Line 708: double minLogDice = Double.parseDouble(params.getOrDefault("min_logdice", "3.0")); — no try/catch
Line 709: int maxPerNoun = Integer.parseInt(params.getOrDefault("max_per_noun", "50")); — no try/catch
Line 786: int maxExamples = Integer.parseInt(params.getOrDefault("max", "10")); — no try/catch
Line 813: int limit = Integer.parseInt(params.getOrDefault("limit", "10")); — no try/catch
Contrast: lines 468-473 in handleSemanticFieldExplore and lines 606-610 in handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti both wrap all parseInt/parseDouble in try/catch(NumberFormatException)
issues.suggestion: Wrap all parseInt/parseDouble calls in handleSemanticField, handleSemanticFieldExamples, and handleConcordanceExamples with try/catch(NumberFormatException) that returns 400 with an error message, consistent with sibling handlers.

issues.summary: getTotalFrequency silently swallows lookup exceptions and returns 0, causing callers to compute incorrect logDice scores without any failure signal
evidence: getTotalFrequency: catch (Exception e) { logger.warn("Failed to retrieve frequency..."); return 0; }
LogDiceCalculator.compute: when headwordFreq or collocateTotal is 0, returns 0.0 — callers receive a logDice of 0.0 and cannot tell whether the word is rare or the lookup failed
buildAndRankCollocates calls getTotalFrequency per collocate in a loop — a systematic failure would silently zero out all scores
issues.suggestion: Either propagate the IOException from getTotalFrequency (declaring it throws IOException), or have the catch return a negative sentinel and document that callers must treat negatives as 'unknown'; do not silently return a valid-looking 0.

issues.summary: BlackLabConllUIndexer.errorOccurred returns true to continue indexing but callers have no way to discover how many files failed
evidence: errorOccurred: logger.error("Indexing error in {}: {}", path, e.getMessage()); return true; // continue indexing
documentCount and tokenCount are incremented in fileDone, but there is no errorCount AtomicLong — callers only see total success counts, not failure counts
getDocumentCount() and getTokenCount() are public; there is no getErrorCount() method
issues.suggestion: Add an AtomicLong errorCount field, increment it in errorOccurred, and expose getErrorCount() so callers can assert or log how many files were skipped on errors.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser retains ~8 @Deprecated methods alongside the clean replacement API, creating a two-API island in one class
evidence: @Deprecated extractCollocateFromSnippet — replaced by extractCollocateLemma / extractLemmaAt
@Deprecated extractCollocateFromMatch — replaced by extractLemmaAt
@Deprecated extractCollocateFromPlainText — replaced by extractLemmaAt
@Deprecated extractTokenFromSnippet — replaced by extractLemmaAt
The file section comments '// ==================== Consolidated clean API ====================' mark the boundary but the deprecated section remains

Deprecated methods are package-private; callers in the same package (BlackLabQueryExecutor) still invoke extractCollocateFromSnippet and extractCollocateFromMatch via extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and executeSurfacePattern
issues.suggestion: Migrate the remaining callers in BlackLabQueryExecutor.extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and executeSurfacePattern to use extractLemmaAt or extractCollocateLemma, then delete the deprecated methods. This removes the dual-API island and reduces ambiguity for future contributors.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer houses 6 inner result DTOs that are imported and consumed by WordSketchApiServer, diverging from the flat-class convention used elsewhere
evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java imports: SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, SemanticFieldExplorer.AdjectiveProfile, SemanticFieldExplorer.ExplorationResult, SemanticFieldExplorer.DiscoveredNoun, SemanticFieldExplorer.CoreCollocate, SemanticFieldExplorer.ExploreOptions

All other DTOs in the codebase are top-level classes (QueryResults.WordSketchResult, QueryResults.ConcordanceResult, RadialPlot.Item) — inner-class DTOs in SemanticFieldExplorer are the sole outlier pattern
The SemanticFieldExplorer class Javadoc acknowledges: 'These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585)'
issues.suggestion: Create a query/model/ or query/results/ sub-package and extract ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, and ExploreOptions as top-level classes, consistent with QueryResults.*.

issues.summary: HTTP handler logic is split between WordSketchApiServer (public) and SketchHandlers (package-private) without a clear organizing principle, diverging from a single-class-per-concern convention
evidence: WordSketchApiServer contains: handleSketchRequest, handleSurfaceRelations, handleDepRelations, handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery, handleRelationQueryInternal, handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticField, handleSemanticFieldExamples, handleConcordanceExamples, handleVisualRadial, handleBcqlQueryPost

SketchHandlers also contains: handleSketchRequest, handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery, handleRelationQueryInternal, handleConcordanceExamples, handleVisualRadial, handleBcqlQueryPost
The methods have the same names in both files but SketchHandlers is package-private — no documentation explains which file is authoritative or the refactoring trajectory
issues.suggestion: Document the intended split (e.g., WordSketchApiServer owns server lifecycle and routing; SketchHandlers owns request handling) or complete the extraction and have WordSketchApiServer delegate all handle* methods to SketchHandlers.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer and SketchHandlers have zero test coverage despite containing ~700 lines of HTTP handler logic including parameter validation, error handling, and JSON serialization
evidence: No test file exists under src/test/java/.../api/ for WordSketchApiServer or SketchHandlers
WordSketchApiServer contains 15+ handler methods including resolveRelationAlias (which maps public aliases to internal IDs) with no tests
Integer.parseInt/Double.parseDouble without NFE guards at lines 708-709, 786, 813 have no test asserting 400 response on invalid input
CORS and OPTIONS preflight logic in registerGetHandler is untested
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests using a mock QueryExecutor and mock HttpExchange (or an embedded HttpServer) to cover at minimum: valid requests return 200, missing required params return 400, invalid numeric params return 400, and relation alias resolution is correct. The StubExecutor pattern from SemanticFieldExplorerTest is directly applicable.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor has no unit tests for its core logic (findCollocations, executeSurfacePattern, buildAndRankCollocates), leaving the primary query path untested without a live index
evidence: BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest and BlackLabDummyTest are integration tests that open a real BlackLab index — they are skipped or fail in environments without the index
buildAndRankCollocates (private helper that applies logDice filtering and sorting) has no dedicated test path exercisable without BlackLab
The collocate extraction logic (extractCollocateFromMatch, extractCollocateLemma fallback chain) in findCollocations is only reachable through a live index
issues.suggestion: Extract buildAndRankCollocates to a package-private or protected scope and add a unit test that exercises it with a pre-built frequency map, a mock getTotalFrequency, and specific minLogDice thresholds. The collocate extraction can be tested via the existing BlackLabSnippetParser unit tests.

issues.summary: RadialPlot SVG generator has no tests despite having non-trivial spiral layout math and XML escaping logic
evidence: src/test/java/.../viz/ directory exists but is empty — no test class for RadialPlot
RadialPlot.toSVG() computes spiral positions, scales constants by canvas size, escapes XML, and has a signed-mode color branch — all untested paths
escapeXml: replaces &, <, >, ", ' — incorrect escaping would produce invalid SVG silently
issues.suggestion: Add a RadialPlotTest that: (1) asserts toSVG() produces valid XML for a known item list, (2) asserts escapeXml handles special characters, (3) asserts signed-mode generates different fill colors than unsigned mode.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor interface uses 'headword' in findCollocations and 'lemma' in executeSurfacePattern for the same concept — inconsistent parameter naming across sibling interface methods
evidence: QueryExecutor.findCollocations signature: findCollocations(String headword, String cqlPattern, double minLogDice, int maxResults)
QueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern signature: executeSurfacePattern(String lemma, String bcqlPattern, int headPosition, int collocatePosition, double minLogDice, int maxResults)
BlackLabQueryExecutor.findDependencyCollocations also uses 'headword' — three slightly different names (headword, lemma, headword) for the same concept across the same class
issues.suggestion: Standardize on 'lemma' throughout the QueryExecutor interface and BlackLabQueryExecutor implementation, since the codebase consistently uses 'lemma' elsewhere (LongIntHashMap key is lemma, GrammarConfigLoader refers to lemmas, LogDiceCalculator docs say lemma).

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.findDependencyCollocations is a public method that does not appear on the QueryExecutor interface, creating an undocumented API extension that bypasses the contract
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor has: public List<QueryResults.WordSketchResult> findDependencyCollocations(String headword, String deprel, double minLogDice, int maxResults) throws IOException
QueryExecutor interface does not declare findDependencyCollocations

WordSketchApiServer uses executor (typed as QueryExecutor) — to call findDependencyCollocations it must cast to BlackLabQueryExecutor, breaking the interface abstraction (or it cannot call it at all through the interface)
issues.suggestion: Add findDependencyCollocations to the QueryExecutor interface with appropriate Javadoc, or make the method package-private if it is only called within the query package.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern has 6 parameters; the ExploreOptions record pattern already in use for SemanticFieldExplorer should be applied here
evidence: executeSurfacePattern(String lemma, String bcqlPattern, int headPosition, int collocatePosition, double minLogDice, int maxResults) — 6 parameters

SemanticFieldExplorer already has ExploreOptions record bundling headPos, collocatePos, topCollocates, minLogDice, minShared, nounsPerCollocate — demonstrating the codebase is aware of the >5-parameter problem
Callers in WordSketchApiServer and SemanticFieldExplorer must pass headPosition and collocatePosition as separate ints, which are positionally adjacent and easy to swap silently
issues.suggestion: Define a SurfacePatternOptions record (or reuse ExploreOptions fields) bundling headPosition, collocatePosition, minLogDice, and maxResults; update the interface method to accept (String lemma, String bcqlPattern, SurfacePatternOptions opts).

issues.summary: Missing required input parameters produce inconsistent responses across sibling endpoints: some return 400, others return an empty/partial result
evidence: handleConcordanceExamples: if (word1 == null || word1.isEmpty() || word2 == null || word2.isEmpty()) { HttpApiUtils.sendError(exchange, 400, "Missing required parameters"); return; } — returns 400
handleSemanticFieldExplore: if (seed == null || seed.isBlank()) returns ExplorationResult.empty(seed) — the handler returns 200 with an empty result instead of 400
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: if seeds.length < 2 returns 400 — consistent with concordance examples but not with single-seed explore
A caller sending a blank seed to /api/semantic-field/explore receives a 200 with empty results and no indication that the input was invalid
issues.suggestion: Standardize: return 400 with a descriptive error message whenever a required parameter is missing or blank, across all endpoints. Update handleSemanticFieldExplore to check seed.isBlank() and return 400 before calling the explorer.

issues.summary: The HTTP server binds to all network interfaces (0.0.0.0) rather than localhost, making the unauthenticated API reachable from other hosts on the network
evidence: server = com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer.create(new InetSocketAddress(port), 0) — InetSocketAddress(port) binds to the wildcard address (0.0.0.0), not InetSocketAddress(InetAddress.getLoopbackAddress(), port)
The API has no authentication and passes BCQL queries directly to the index parser — reachable from any host on the LAN with no guard
CLAUDE.md and README.md describe the server as a local tool; the binding does not match the intended trust boundary
issues.suggestion: Change to: new InetSocketAddress(InetAddress.getLoopbackAddress(), port) or new InetSocketAddress("127.0.0.1", port) to restrict to localhost. If external access is needed, document it explicitly and note the no-auth implication.
